method_atom: A high-dimensional integral (partition function) over variables constrained to satisfy local closedness/flatness conditions on the cells of a combinatorial cell complex, whose degree of divergence (its volume scaling) is not captured by a naive count of elementary substructures but is instead governed by a topological/cohomological invariant of the complex — a Betti number. This identification holds exactly under simplifying conditions on the underlying symmetry structure (a commutative/Abelian group) or on the complex's connectivity (a trivial fundamental group), and only approximately otherwise.
object_atom: Lattice topological field theories whose dynamical variables are flat discrete connections with compact structure group on a cell 2-complex — including the weak-coupling limit of 2d Yang-Mills theory, the Ponzano-Regge model of 3d quantum gravity, and discrete BF theory — together with the ill-defined path-integral measure that produces their 'bubble divergences'.
brief_reason: What carries across is that the divergence degree of a constrained integral over a cell complex is set by a cohomological invariant (a Betti number) rather than a naive substructure count; what is domain-specific is the flat-connection lattice gauge/gravity path integral and its bubble divergences.
